snippets.title: Peter Yang on agents and coding
snippets.takeaway: So let me get this straight: 1. We publish frontier models 2. They get distilled into cheap open source models 3.
snippets.summary: Peter Yang (petergyang on X) shared 3 recent updates. So let me get this straight: 1. We publish frontier models 2. They get distilled into cheap open source models 3. US companies adopt the same open source models because they're good enough and much cheaper 4. We start gating access to frontier models What next? US companies innovate less? Open source models become more attractive?

snippets.title: Guillermo Rauch on agents and models
snippets.takeaway: The UI for AI is here. It's @shadcn Agents are particularly hard-to-debug software. For one, and by design, AI models behave in non-deterministic ways.
snippets.summary: Guillermo Rauch (rauchg on X) shared 3 recent updates. The UI for AI is here. It's @shadcn Agents are particularly hard-to-debug software. For one, and by design, AI models behave in non-deterministic ways. Even two identical prompts don't always yield the same output. But agents are also complex distributed systems. They involve multiple steps of computation across functions and sandboxes, touching dozens of API services that can go down, rate limit you, etc.

snippets.takeaway: Written for the seed founder who’s building something really important in a not-so-hot category.. I see you 🙏 Now that the dust is settled, what’s really funny to me about the...
snippets.summary: Nikunj Kothari (nikunj on X) shared 2 recent updates. Written for the seed founder who’s building something really important in a not-so-hot category.. I see you 🙏 Now that the dust is settled, what’s really funny to me about the taste commentary is it’s coming from a LOT of people who never have built anything.. You can’t achieve (or refine) taste without being in the arena. It’s like a chef cooking and expecting their first dish to be good on the first go.

snippets.title: Dan Shipper on coding and models
snippets.takeaway: BREAKING: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 Sol! As of today, by U.S. government directive, access is limited to only ~20 pre-approved companies and @every is not on the list.
snippets.summary: Dan Shipper (danshipper on X) shared 2 recent updates. BREAKING: OpenAI announced GPT-5.6 Sol! As of today, by U.S. government directive, access is limited to only ~20 pre-approved companies and @every is not on the list. This appears to be a temporary situation while the government races to figure out a long-term policy for releasing frontier models with advanced capabilities.

snippets.title: Aditya Agarwal on agents
snippets.takeaway: An interesting side effect of AI is that I have zero tolerance for shallow interactions with humans. I really deep connection and depth in my relationships.
snippets.summary: Aditya Agarwal (adityaag on X) shared 1 recent update. An interesting side effect of AI is that I have zero tolerance for shallow interactions with humans. I really deep connection and depth in my relationships. More ways to do that are so valuable. And for everything else: give me agents. I suspect our world will become both smaller and more rich in it's relationship depth.

snippets.title: Why Traditional Benchmarks Fail Modern AI Models with OpenAI Research Scientist Noam Brown
snippets.takeaway: Why Traditional Benchmarks Fail Modern AI Models with OpenAI Research Scientist Noam Brown. If you can build an agent or control the amount of test time compute being used, like,...
snippets.summary: If you can build an agent or control the amount of test time compute being used, like, there's what is done by the model itself, and there's what the user can do. We all need to actually reinforce either, like, a patience limit or a budget limit from a token perspective now, and that wasn't true a few years ago. Like, if you gave it a budget of $10,000,000 and said, okay.
